---
title: "Orientation Day Mentor Runbook"
---

## Purpose

This is a mentor-facing checklist for running the first orientation meeting.

## Before the meeting

- Prepare the orientation deck.
- Open this website locally or on GitHub Pages.
- Prepare one whiteboard diagram of the full pipeline.
- Check student background: R, biology, RNA-seq, statistics.

## Suggested agenda

| Time | Segment | Goal |
|---|---|---|
| 0-5 min | Welcome | Explain that today is a map session |
| 5-15 min | Bulk limitation | Show why averages hide heterogeneity |
| 15-25 min | Why scRNA-seq | Connect to rare cells and disease biology |
| 25-40 min | Technology overview | Explain barcodes, UMIs, droplet methods |
| 40-50 min | Count matrix | Make students explain rows and columns |
| 50-65 min | Computational pipeline | Preview QC, UMAP, clustering, annotation |
| 65-72 min | Advanced preview | DE, pseudotime, integration, pitfalls |
| 72-75 min | Day 0 bridge | Explain R setup and homework |

## Key mentor phrases

> Today is not a coding session. Today is a map session.

> UMAP is not the raw data. The raw object we care about is the gene-by-cell count matrix.

> Running code is Level 1. Understanding what each step does is Level 2. Knowing whether the biology makes sense is Level 3.

## Discussion questions

1. What information can be hidden inside an average expression profile?
2. Why might a gene have a zero count in one cell?
3. Why can doublets create fake cell states?
4. Why should we be careful when naming clusters?

## End-of-meeting checklist

- Students can explain bulk vs single-cell.
- Students can describe barcode and UMI at a high level.
- Students can identify the count matrix.
- Students can draw the analysis workflow.
- Students know what to install for Day 0.
